article_id: Article 223. Regulation of the monetary and financial system
text_snippet: The regulation of the monetary and financial system of the Nation is the responsibility of the Monetary Board as the superior organ of the Central Bank.

article_id: Article 224. Integration of the Monetary Board
text_snippet: The Monetary Board is integrated by no more than nine members including the Governor of the Central Bank, who presides over it, and ex officio members whose number shall not be more than three.
The Central Bank of the Republic is an entity of Public Right with juridical personality, its own patrimony, and functional, budgetary, and administrative autonomy.

article_id: Article 228. Issue of bills and coins
text_snippet: The Central Bank, whose capital is property of the State, is the sole issuer of bills and coins of national circulation and has as its objective to watch for the stability of prices.
The national monetary unit is the Dominican Peso.

article_id: Article 23. Loss of the rights of citizenship
text_snippet: The rights of citizenship are lost by irrevocable condemnation in cases of treason, espionage, conspiracy as well as for taking up arms and for aiding or participating in attempts of deliberate damage

article_id: Article 230. Legal force and tender of the monetary unit
text_snippet: Only bills issues and coins minted by the Central Bank shall have legal circulation and tender, under the unlimited guarantee of the State and in the proportions and conditions indicated by law.

article_id: Article 231. Prohibition of the issuance of monetary signs
text_snippet: The issuance of paper, coins, or other monetary signs not authorized by this Constitution is prohibited.

article_id: Article 32. The National Coat of Arms

The National Hymn is the musical composition of José Reyes with the lyrics of Emilio Prud’Homme, and it is unique and invariable.
The National Motto is “God, Country, and Liberty”.
The 27th of February and 16th of August, anniversaries of the Independence and the Restoration of the Republic, respectively, are declared national holidays.
